content: The inbox uses a pending/done directory structure. New messages land in pending/, and the processor moves them to done/ after handling. The directory location is the status — no flag scanning, no rewriting. The move is atomic (mv).

provenance.raw_agent:
Good call. Here's how I'd structure it:

.pi/inbox/
  pending/   ← new messages land here
  done/      ← processor moves them here after handling

One file per message, named {timestamp}-{uuid}.jsonl so they're naturally time-ordered. The processor can: 1. List pending/ 2. Pick up a file 3. Process it 4. mv pending/{file} done/{file} — atomic, no rewriting, no flag scanning
provenance.raw_user: I think pending and done might be best
provenance.what_was_ratified: Inbox uses pending/done directory structure where pending/ holds new messages and done/ holds processed messages. The directory is the status — atomic mv from pending to done, no flag scanning or file rewriting.
